+{"id":"bd-0vmg6","title":"port ext4 data_err=abort mount-option parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:26:15.196010910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T15:00:32.874276351Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T15:00:32.872089814Z","close_reason":"Implemented ext4 data_err mount-option semantics with abort remount-ro behavior and mount_options coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-0ypb","title":"Publish criterion benchmark baselines with regression detection","description":"## What
Refresh criterion benchmark baselines that are ~2 months stale (latest: 2026-02-19) and ensure
the existing perf-regression CI workflow runs cleanly against current code.

## Why
Benchmark infrastructure is comprehensive — 10 benchmark files exist across 6 crates, a
scripts/benchmark_record.sh script exists, and .github/workflows/perf-regression.yml provides
CI integration. However, baselines haven't been refreshed since February 2026. Two months of
active development (100+ commits) means the baselines are meaningless for regression detection.

## What Already Exists
- 10 benchmark files: arc_cache, ondisk_parse, metadata_parse, extent_resolve, mount_runtime,
  degraded_pressure, wal_throughput, bitmap_ops, batch_alloc, scrub_codec
- scripts/benchmark_record.sh with hyperfine integration
- baselines/ directory with historical results (latest: 2026-02-19)
- benchmarks/baselines/latest.json
- .github/workflows/perf-regression.yml (manual dispatch)

## Remaining Work
1. Run all 10 existing criterion benchmarks on release-perf profile
2. Run scripts/benchmark_record.sh to capture new hyperfine baselines
3. Commit updated baselines to baselines/ and benchmarks/baselines/
4. Verify perf-regression.yml workflow runs cleanly (manual dispatch)
5. If any benchmarks fail to compile (API drift from 2 months of changes), fix them
6. Document current numbers in baselines/baseline-20260406.md

## Test Plan
- All 10 benchmark binaries compile on release-perf profile
- scripts/benchmark_record.sh completes without errors
- perf-regression.yml passes on manual dispatch
- Variance < 5% across 3 consecutive benchmark runs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All existing benchmarks compile and run
- [ ] Fresh baselines committed (baselines/baseline-20260406.md)
- [ ] benchmarks/baselines/latest.json updated
- [ ] perf-regression.yml verified working
- [ ] Any API-drift compilation failures in benchmarks fixed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T15:50:14.522069618Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:04:56.438665958Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:04:56.438256503Z","close_reason":"Baselines refreshed 2026-04-06: baseline-20260406.md with release-perf profile, latest.json updated, perf-regression.yml has --profile release-perf","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1054","title":"Add checked arithmetic for block allocation casts in ffs-alloc","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-23T05:40:59.820511639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-23T05:52:02.490103010Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T05:52:02.490083043Z","close_reason":"Hardened all three dangerous as-u32 casts in ffs-alloc: from_superblock group_count uses unwrap_or(MAX), free_inode and free_inode_persist group_idx use try_from with proper error propagation. Remaining casts are guarded by range checks or bounded by filesystem geometry.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-109v","title":"Fix stale asupersync/frankentui on rch remote workers causing build failures","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-20T00:42:07.379679416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T01:53:11.657278404Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T01:53:11.657259058Z","close_reason":"Fixed by bumping asupersync to 0.2.5 and ftui to 0.2.1 from crates.io, removing stale path overrides. All rch workers now build successfully.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
